# PaintKiDukaan — Implementation Plan

**Generated by**: Lead Sisyphus (hyperplan distillation, plan-agent delegated due to environmental constraints)
**Source bundle**: [.omo/plans/paint-shop-insight-bundle.md](./paint-shop-insight-bundle.md)
**Status**: DRAFT — PENDING USER INTAKE

---

## ⚠️ STOP — Read this first

Before any code is written, the user MUST answer the **15 intake questions** in [§5 of the bundle](./paint-shop-insight-bundle.md#5-mandatory-user-intake-15-q-by-axis-before-any-code). This plan proceeds with **safe defaults** explicitly marked `[PENDING-DEFAULT]` so development can begin in parallel, but the user MUST confirm or override each before v1 release.

**Defaults assumed below** (override with intake answers):
- Single shop, 1 location
- 1 owner + 1 cashier (2 staff)
- Pre-mixed tins only (NO counter tinting)
- ml + L UoM (no gal/kg)
- Primer/putty stocked (batch + expiry required)
- B2B contractors, udhaar/credit workflow active
- No quotations/WhatsApp in v1
- USB barcode scanner (HID keyboard mode)
- 58mm thermal printer
- Cloud-only (no offline)
- GST registered, turnover < ₹5Cr (no e-invoice)
- No legacy migration

---

## Stack (locked from §1)

```
Next.js 14 (App Router)  +  Postgres  +  Prisma  +  Tailwind  +  bwip-js
```

**No Electron for v1.** Gated by Q14.
**No SQLite for v1.** Postgres required for F3 concurrency + F5 regulatory schema.

---

## v1 Scope — Non-Negotiable (from §2)

| # | Primitive | Phase |
|---|-----------|-------|
| F1 | Entity boundaries: Product, Barcode, StockMovement, Purchase, PurchaseItem, Sale, SaleItem, Customer, Invoice | Phase 1 |
| F2 | StockMovement append-only ledger; stock = SUM(movements) | Phase 1 |
| F3 | Customer spending derived from invoices | Phase 1 |
| F4 | Barcode lifecycle: Code128 internal SKU + uniqueness + label print + scanner lookup + reprint | Phase 2 |
| F5 | Indian regulatory: HSN, CGST/SGST/IGST, sequential FY invoice numbering | Phase 1 |
| F6 | Concurrency: Postgres advisory locks + SELECT FOR UPDATE + idempotency key | Phase 3 |
| F7 | Returns/void in v1: reason codes + approver + audit row | Phase 4 |
| F8 | Udhaar ledger: receivables, partial payments, aging buckets | Phase 4 |
| F9 | 2-role auth: admin + cashier | Phase 1 |

**Gated (from §3) — only build if intake confirms**:
- Counter tinting: `tint_required: boolean` flag; if false, no tint schema
- Multi-UoM (ml/L/gal/kg): always build, even if user sells ml-only (defensive)
- Batch + expiry: always build (primers/putty exist per default)

---

## v2 Scope — DEFERRED (from §4)

Build AFTER intake confirms OR after v1 ships. **Do NOT include in v1 plan tasks.**

| Feature | Intake gate |
|---|---|
| Multi-location / godown | Q1 |
| WhatsApp/SMS sharing | Q11 |
| Sales orders / quotations | Q10 |
| Vendor price lists | (none in current 15-Q; add to v2) |
| E-invoicing | Turnover > ₹5Cr |
| Offline sync + RPO/RTO | Q14 |
| Full RBAC (>2 roles) | Q2 (staff > 2) |
| Tally/Marg migration | (add to v2) |
| GSTR-1/3B export | (regulatory gap; plan as separate adapter) |

---

## Phased Execution Plan

### Phase 0 — Repo Bootstrap (½ day)

**Goal**: Greenfield Next.js 14 project skeleton with the locked stack.

**Deliverables**:
- `npx create-next-app@14 paintkidujaan --typescript --tailwind --app`
- Add Prisma + Postgres connection
- Add bwip-js dependency
- Add shadcn/ui (for consistent UI primitives)
- Initial Prisma schema file (`prisma/schema.prisma`) with all F1 entities, even if not all are exercised yet
- `.env.example` with `DATABASE_URL`
- `pnpm dev` (or `npm run dev`) starts cleanly
- Health check route `/api/health` returns 200

**Acceptance**:
- `pnpm dev` runs without errors
- `prisma migrate dev` creates the initial schema
- `curl localhost:3000/api/health` returns `{ status: "ok" }`

**Dependencies**: None.

---

### Phase 1 — Domain Core (CRUD + Audit + Auth) (2-3 days)

**Goal**: All F1-F3, F5, F9 primitives in DB and API. No UI polish, no barcode, no POS yet.

**Deliverables**:

1. **Prisma schema** (`prisma/schema.prisma`):
   - `User` (id, email, password_hash, role: enum{ADMIN, CASHIER}, created_at)
   - `Product` (id, sku, name, hsn_code, gst_rate, base_uom, units_per_base, brand, category, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
   - `ProductUom` (id, product_id, uom: enum{ml, L, gal, kg, pcs}, conversion_to_base: decimal, is_default)
   - `Barcode` (id, product_id, value: string UNIQUE, format: enum{CODE128, EAN13}, source: enum{INTERNAL, MANUFACTURER}, created_at)
   - `StockMovement` (id, product_id, batch_id: nullable, quantity_delta: decimal, reason: enum{PURCHASE, SALE, RETURN, ADJUSTMENT, EXPIRY}, reference_id: nullable, user_id, created_at) — **append-only, no UPDATE/DELETE in app layer**
   - `Batch` (id, product_id, lot_number, manufactured_at, expires_at, created_at) — only created if batch tracking enabled
   - `Purchase` (id, supplier_name, supplier_gstin, invoice_number, invoice_date, total, gst_total, user_id, created_at)
   - `PurchaseItem` (id, purchase_id, product_id, batch_id: nullable, quantity, unit_price, gst_rate, line_total)
   - `Customer` (id, name, phone, address, gstin: nullable, opening_balance: decimal, created_at)
   - `Sale` (id, customer_id: nullable, invoice_number: string UNIQUE, invoice_date, subtotal, gst_total, discount, total, payment_mode: enum{CASH, UPI, CARD, CREDIT, SPLIT}, user_id, created_at)
   - `SaleItem` (id, sale_id, product_id, batch_id: nullable, quantity, unit_price, gst_rate, line_total)
   - `Payment` (id, customer_id, sale_id: nullable, amount, mode: enum{CASH, UPI, CARD}, reference, user_id, created_at)
   - `CreditLedger` (id, customer_id, sale_id: nullable, payment_id: nullable, debit: decimal, credit: decimal, balance_after: decimal, created_at) — derived view, append-only
   - `AuditLog` (id, user_id, action, entity, entity_id, before: jsonb, after: jsonb, created_at) — **immutable, no UPDATE/DELETE**
   - `InvoiceSequence` (fy: string, last_number: int) — used for sequential FY numbering

2. **Auth** (F9):
   - NextAuth.js with email+password, JWT session
   - 2 hardcoded roles enforced in middleware
   - `app/(auth)/login` page

3. **API routes** (REST under `app/api/`):
   - `POST /api/auth/login`, `POST /api/auth/logout`
   - `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/products`
   - `GET/POST /api/purchases`
   - `GET/POST /api/sales` (skeleton — POS comes in Phase 3)
   - `GET/POST /api/customers`
   - `GET /api/inventory` (returns SUM of StockMovement per product)

4. **Audit middleware**: any mutation in `app/api/**/route.ts` writes an AuditLog row. Centralize in a `withAudit()` wrapper.

5. **Sequential invoice numbering** (F5): on Sale creation, transaction locks `InvoiceSequence` row for the current FY, increments, releases. Zero gaps guaranteed.

**Acceptance**:
- `prisma migrate dev` succeeds
- Can create a Product via API
- Can create a Purchase with multiple PurchaseItems; StockMovement records are written
- Can create a Customer with opening_balance
- Can fetch `/api/inventory` and see SUM of all stock movements
- Attempt to UPDATE a StockMovement row in DB raises a trigger error (append-only enforcement)
- AuditLog has rows for every mutation

**Dependencies**: Phase 0.

---

### Phase 2 — Barcode Lifecycle (F4) (1-2 days)

**Goal**: Generate Code128 barcodes for products, print labels, lookup at scan.

**Deliverables**:

1. **Barcode generation**:
   - `POST /api/products/:id/barcodes` generates a new Code128 from the product's internal SKU (deterministic, no random seed)
   - Server-side validation: barcode value must be unique across all `Barcode` rows
   - Conflict policy: if manufacturer EAN is already linked to another product, return 409 with options (re-print, override, reject)

2. **Label print**:
   - `GET /api/products/:id/labels?count=N` returns a print-ready HTML/PDF using bwip-js + 58mm thermal layout
   - Frontend route `/inventory/labels?productId=X` shows print button
   - Browser print dialog with `window.print()` and `@media print` CSS for 58mm/80mm widths

3. **Reprint workflow**:
   - `GET /api/products/:id/barcodes` returns existing barcodes
   - Reprint is a 1-click duplicate of the same `value` (no regeneration)

4. **Scanner lookup** (preparation for Phase 3):
   - `GET /api/scan/:barcode` returns `{ product, default_uom, price, available_stock }` in <50ms
   - Uses `Barcode.value` index, joins Product + inventory SUM

**Acceptance**:
- `POST /api/products/abc/barcodes` returns `{ value: "PKD-abc-001", format: "CODE128" }`
- Printing the label page produces a scannable Code128 label
- Scanning the label with a USB HID scanner returns the right product via the lookup endpoint
- Conflict case (duplicate EAN) returns 409 with structured error

**Dependencies**: Phase 1.

---

### Phase 3 — POS (Sales Page + Concurrency) (F6) (2-3 days)

**Goal**: The 4th page (`/sales`) is the actual point-of-sale. Scanner-as-keyboard. Concurrency-safe.

**Deliverables**:

1. **POS UI** (`app/(dashboard)/sales/page.tsx`):
   - Search/scan input field with autofocus (USB HID scanners type + Enter)
   - Cart table: product name, qty, UoM, unit price, line total, remove button
   - Customer picker (or "Walk-in")
   - Payment mode selector (CASH / UPI / CARD / CREDIT / SPLIT)
   - Live total: subtotal, GST split (CGST + SGST or IGST), grand total
   - "Bill" button → creates Sale, prints invoice, clears cart

2. **Concurrency primitives** (F6):
   - `POST /api/sales` is wrapped in a Prisma transaction
   - On insert: `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the product row(s)
   - StockMovement row written with negative quantity_delta
   - Idempotency key: client sends `Idempotency-Key` header (UUID); server stores it; duplicate requests return the original Sale (no double-decrement)
   - Optimistic UI: if 409 returned, show "Stock changed — refresh cart" toast

3. **Invoice print** (F5):
   - After Sale created, auto-open print dialog with GST-compliant invoice
   - Sequential FY number on every invoice (no manual entry)
   - CGST/SGST split (intra-state) or IGST (inter-state) based on customer state vs shop state

4. **Reprint**:
   - Sale detail page has "Reprint" button → same invoice template

**Acceptance**:
- Scan a barcode → product appears in cart
- Two POS sessions scanning the same low-stock item simultaneously → only one succeeds (the other gets 409)
- Bill creates Invoice with sequential number, writes AuditLog, prints
- Same `Idempotency-Key` retried → returns same Sale, not duplicate
- Credit sale updates customer's CreditLedger balance

**Dependencies**: Phase 1, Phase 2.

---

### Phase 4 — Returns, Credit, Batch (F7, F8) (1-2 days)

**Goal**: Real-world operating model — udhaar, returns, expired stock.

**Deliverables**:

1. **Returns** (F7):
   - `POST /api/sales/:id/return` creates a Return record with reason code (WRONG_SHADE, DAMAGED, CUSTOMER_CHANGE_MIND, OTHER)
   - Refund mode: CASH, UPI, CREDIT (khata credit)
   - Reason + approver user_id recorded in AuditLog
   - StockMovement written with positive quantity_delta (re-stocking) UNLESS reason = DAMAGED
   - If batch tracking enabled: links to original batch for same-shade return

2. **Void / cancellation**:
   - `POST /api/sales/:id/void` cancels an unpaid Sale (reason + approver)
   - Reverses any stock reservation
   - Invoice number still consumed (no gap, but marked VOID)

3. **Udhaar ledger** (F8):
   - `GET /api/customers/:id/ledger` returns running balance + aging buckets (0-30d, 31-60d, 61-90d, 90+d)
   - `POST /api/customers/:id/payments` records partial payment
   - CreditLedger is append-only; balance = SUM(debit) - SUM(credit)

4. **Batch + expiry view**:
   - `GET /api/inventory?expiredWithin=30` returns batches expiring in next 30 days
   - `GET /api/inventory?lowStock=true&threshold=N` returns low-stock products
   - Both are derived queries on StockMovement + Batch — no notifications infrastructure (per researcher R1)

**Acceptance**:
- Return a sold item → stock restored, refund recorded, AuditLog entry
- Void an unpaid bill → invoice number consumed, no StockMovement reversal (none existed)
- Customer with ₹5,000 outstanding + ₹2,000 payment shows balance ₹3,000 with aging breakdown
- Expiring batches visible in inventory list

**Dependencies**: Phase 1, Phase 3.

---

### Phase 5 — Hardening (½ day per item)

**Goal**: Cross-cutting concerns that protect against month-1 breakage.

**Deliverables** (each is independently shippable):

1. **Error boundaries** on every page (`app/error.tsx` + `app/(dashboard)/error.tsx`)
2. **Rate limiting** on `/api/sales` (per-user token bucket)
3. **Database backup** script (`scripts/backup.sh`) — daily `pg_dump` to local + optional S3
4. **Health check** extended: DB ping, disk space, recent error count
5. **Migration script** if user confirms Tally/Busy/Excel import in intake (gated)
6. **Seed data** for demo: 20 sample products, 5 customers, 1 user
7. **README** with run instructions, env vars, intake answers as a "Configuration" section

**Acceptance**:
- `/api/health` returns DB status
- Backup script produces a restorable dump
- Seed data loads without errors

**Dependencies**: Phase 1+.

---

### Phase 6 — Pre-ship Gate (½ day)

**Goal**: User acceptance + sign-off.

**Deliverables**:
- Run the application end-to-end with the user's data
- Walk through the 4 pages
- Demo barcode scan → sale → invoice
- Demo return → refund → ledger
- Collect feedback
- Fix any P0/P1 bugs
- Document any deferred items as v2 backlog

**Acceptance**:
- User confirms "ready to use"
- All intake answers confirmed or defaults overridden
- v2 backlog documented

**Dependencies**: Phases 1-5.

---

## Critical Files (planned structure)

```
paintkidujaan/
├── prisma/
│   ├── schema.prisma              # Phase 1 — F1, F2, F3, F5, F9
│   ├── migrations/
│   └── seed.ts                    # Phase 5
├── app/
│   ├── (auth)/
│   │   └── login/page.tsx
│   ├── (dashboard)/
│   │   ├── layout.tsx             # 2-role sidebar
│   │   ├── sales/page.tsx         # Phase 3 — POS
│   │   ├── purchases/page.tsx     # Phase 1
│   │   ├── customers/page.tsx     # Phase 1
│   │   ├── inventory/page.tsx     # Phase 1 + Phase 4 expiry/low-stock
│   │   └── reports/page.tsx       # Phase 5 (deferred)
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts
│   │   ├── products/route.ts
│   │   ├── products/[id]/barcodes/route.ts
│   │   ├── products/[id]/labels/route.ts
│   │   ├── scan/[barcode]/route.ts
│   │   ├── sales/route.ts
│   │   ├── sales/[id]/return/route.ts
│   │   ├── sales/[id]/void/route.ts
│   │   ├── sales/[id]/reprint/route.ts
│   │   ├── purchases/route.ts
│   │   ├── customers/route.ts
│   │   ├── customers/[id]/ledger/route.ts
│   │   ├── customers/[id]/payments/route.ts
│   │   ├── inventory/route.ts
│   │   └── health/route.ts
│   ├── error.tsx
│   └── layout.tsx
├── lib/
│   ├── db.ts                      # Prisma client
│   ├── auth.ts                    # NextAuth config
│   ├── audit.ts                   # withAudit() wrapper
│   ├── invoice-sequence.ts        # F5 sequential numbering
│   ├── barcode.ts                 # Code128 generation
│   ├── gst.ts                     # CGST/SGST/IGST calculation
│   └── concurrency.ts             # F6 advisory locks
├── components/
│   ├── pos/                       # Phase 3
│   ├── labels/                    # Phase 2
│   └── ui/                        # shadcn/ui
├── scripts/
│   ├── backup.sh                  # Phase 5
│   └── migrate-tally.ts           # Phase 5 (gated)
├── .env.example
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── README.md
```

---

## Acceptance Criteria Summary (v1)

For v1 to be considered done, ALL of the following must be true:

1. ☐ User has answered (or accepted default for) all 15 intake questions
2. ☐ All 4 pages (Sales, Purchase, Customer, Inventory) functional
3. ☐ Barcode generation → label print → USB scan → product lookup → add to cart → invoice print loop works end-to-end
4. ☐ Concurrent sales don't oversell (advisory locks + idempotency verified)
5. ☐ Returns process restocks correctly with reason + approver
6. ☐ Credit ledger shows correct running balance with aging
7. ☐ GST invoice format compliant: HSN, CGST/SGST/IGST split, sequential FY numbers, no gaps
8. ☐ AuditLog captures all mutations
9. ☐ 2-role auth enforced (admin can override; cashier cannot)
10. ☐ Backup script runs and produces restorable dump
11. ☐ No `as any` or `@ts-ignore` in committed code
12. ☐ LSP diagnostics clean on all committed files
13. ☐ `pnpm build` exits 0
14. ☐ End-to-end demo with real barcode scanner + thermal printer succeeds

---

## v2 Backlog (NOT in v1 plan)

Items the user must opt into via intake or after v1 ships:

- Multi-location (Q1)
- Counter tinting (Q5)
- WhatsApp/SMS invoice sharing (Q11)
- Sales orders / quotations (Q10)
- Vendor price lists + margin tracking
- GSTR-1/3B export adapter
- E-invoicing (turnover > ₹5Cr)
- Offline-first + sync conflict resolution (Q14)
- Full RBAC + price-override approver (Q2 > 2 staff)
- Tally/Busy/Marg import script
- Reports dashboard (Phase 5 — out of MVP scope)
- Multi-language (Hindi UI)
- Mobile responsive (POS is desktop-only in v1)

---

## Open Trade-offs (must be resolved by user)

| # | Trade-off | Resolves on | Default |
|---|-----------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | Electron vs Web | Q14 (offline) | Web on shop PC |
| 2 | Tinting | Q5 (counter mix) | No tint |
| 3 | Multi-location | Q1 (godown) | Single location |
| 4 | WhatsApp sharing | Q11 (customer expectation) | PDF download only |

---

## What this plan does NOT do (per §8 of bundle)

- No UI/UX mockups — UI choices deferred to implementation
- No specific SQL beyond what Prisma generates
- No specific route/component breakdown beyond what's listed (more detail emerges during build)
- No deployment topology — local-only for v1, cloud = Phase 5 hardening
- No vendor/library selection beyond the locked stack

---

## Risk Register (top 5)

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| User answers intake late → default assumptions wrong | Mark all `[PENDING-DEFAULT]` items clearly; check before v1 release |
| Thermal printer driver quirks (macOS, Linux, Windows) | Use browser print + CSS for media; defer driver-level work to v2 |
| Postgres advisory locks on small instance may be noisy | Use Prisma's `$transaction` with `Serializable` isolation as fallback |
| Concurrent cashier + owner same product → race | Idempotency key + advisory lock covers this; test in Phase 3 |
| Indian GST rule changes (rate updates) | HSN + GST rate on Product; central `lib/gst.ts` for calc; update without schema change |

---

**End of plan. Next step: ask the user the 15 intake questions.**
